crime._comment: Any city's Socrata open-data portal. Geocoded but usually published with a lag, so this is the context layer, not live alerts. To use another city: set domain + incidents_dataset to its crime dataset, and map fields to that dataset's column names (the date/offense/address/location-point columns).

cad._comment: Optional real-time calls-for-service layer. Many cities publish a genuinely live dispatch feed (KC's lags, so this is off here). Set enabled=true and point domain + incidents_dataset at your city's live CAD, mapping fields to its columns. The example below is Seattle's live Fire 911 feed. alert=true notifies on new calls near home.

scanner._comment: Broadcastify live audio (real-time alert layer). Free account required; stream URLs are session-gated, so the script logs in to fetch them. Add one or more feeds; each runs its own capture. directory_url is the Broadcastify listing the GUI's feed picker reads — set it to your metro/county page (browse from broadcastify.com/listen). whisper_model: any mlx-community Whisper repo; large-v3-turbo is the accuracy/speed sweet spot for batch scanner audio on Apple Silicon (small.en is lighter but worse on degraded radio). whisper_prompt: optional override for the decoder bias prompt; leave empty to auto-build it from near_streets + priority_keywords.

alerts._comment: near_streets: lowercased street names within/around your radius matched against transcribed dispatch audio. priority_keywords: incident types that always alert regardless of location. notify_command: optional path to your own notifier, called as `<command> <message> <severity> <voice>`; leave empty for native macOS notifications. voice: speak alerts aloud / play alert sound (toggleable in the app).
